tools.name: batchDeleteWorlds.postOperation
tools.description: Deletes one or more worlds in a batch operation.

tools.name: batchDescribeSimulationJob.postOperation
tools.description: Describes one or more simulation jobs.

tools.name: cancelDeploymentJob.postOperation
tools.description:
Cancels the specified deployment job.

This API will no longer be supported as of May 2, 2022. Use it to remove resources that were created for Deployment Service.

tools.name: cancelSimulationJob.postOperation
tools.description: Cancels the specified simulation job.

tools.name: cancelSimulationJobBatch.postOperation
tools.description: Cancels a simulation job batch. When you cancel a simulation job batch, you are also cancelling all of the active simulation jobs created as part of the batch.

tools.name: cancelWorldExportJob.postOperation
tools.description: Cancels the specified export job.

tools.name: cancelWorldGenerationJob.postOperation
tools.description: Cancels the specified world generator job.

tools.name: createDeploymentJob.postOperation
tools.description:
Deploys a specific version of a robot application to robots in a fleet.

This API is no longer supported and will throw an error if used.

The robot application must have a numbered applicationVersion for consistency reasons. To create a new version, use CreateRobotApplicationVersion or see Creating a Robot Application Version.

After 90 days, deployment jobs expire and will be deleted. They will no longer be accessible.

tools.name: createFleet.postOperation
tools.description:
Creates a fleet, a logical group of robots running the same robot application.

This API is no longer supported and will throw an error if used.

tools.name: createRobot.postOperation
tools.description:
Creates a robot.

This API is no longer supported and will throw an error if used.

tools.name: createRobotApplication.postOperation
tools.description: Creates a robot application.

tools.name: createRobotApplicationVersion.postOperation
tools.description: Creates a version of a robot application.

tools.name: createSimulationApplication.postOperation
tools.description: Creates a simulation application.

tools.name: createSimulationApplicationVersion.postOperation
tools.description: Creates a simulation application with a specific revision id.

tools.name: createSimulationJob.postOperation
tools.description:
Creates a simulation job.

After 90 days, simulation jobs expire and will be deleted. They will no longer be accessible.

tools.name: createWorldExportJob.postOperation
tools.description: Creates a world export job.

tools.name: createWorldGenerationJob.postOperation
tools.description: Creates worlds using the specified template.

tools.name: createWorldTemplate.postOperation
tools.description: Creates a world template.

tools.name: listDeploymentJobs.postOperation
tools.description:
Returns a list of deployment jobs for a fleet. You can optionally provide filters to retrieve specific deployment jobs.

This API will no longer be supported as of May 2, 2022. Use it to remove resources that were created for Deployment Service.

tools.name: listFleets.postOperation
tools.description:
Returns a list of fleets. You can optionally provide filters to retrieve specific fleets.

This API will no longer be supported as of May 2, 2022. Use it to remove resources that were created for Deployment Service.

tools.name: listRobotApplications.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of robot application. You can optionally provide filters to retrieve specific robot applications.

tools.name: listRobots.postOperation
tools.description:
Returns a list of robots. You can optionally provide filters to retrieve specific robots.

This API will no longer be supported as of May 2, 2022. Use it to remove resources that were created for Deployment Service.

tools.description: Returns a list of simulation applications. You can optionally provide filters to retrieve specific simulation applications.

tools.name: registerRobot.postOperation
tools.description:
Registers a robot with a fleet.

This API is no longer supported and will throw an error if used.

tools.name: restartSimulationJob.postOperation
tools.description: Restarts a running simulation job.

tools.name: startSimulationJobBatch.postOperation
tools.description: Starts a new simulation job batch. The batch is defined using one or more SimulationJobRequest objects.

tools.name: syncDeploymentJob.postOperation
tools.description:
Syncrhonizes robots in a fleet to the latest deployment. This is helpful if robots were added after a deployment.

This API will no longer be supported as of May 2, 2022. Use it to remove resources that were created for Deployment Service.

tools.name: tags.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Lists all tags on a AWS RoboMaker resource.

tools.name: tags.tagResource
tools.description:
Adds or edits tags for a AWS RoboMaker resource.

Each tag consists of a tag key and a tag value. Tag keys and tag values are both required, but tag values can be empty strings.

For information about the rules that apply to tag keys and tag values, see User-Defined Tag Restrictions in the AWS Billing and Cost Management User Guide.

tools.name: tags.untagResource
tools.description:
Removes the specified tags from the specified AWS RoboMaker resource.

To remove a tag, specify the tag key. To change the tag value of an existing tag key, use TagResource .

tools.description: Updates a robot application.
